channel.title: Asymmetric Intelligence
channel.description: A public intelligence commons. Eight monitors. One weekly briefing each.

item.title: Strategic Conflict & Escalation Monitor — W/E 30 April 2026
item.description: JNIM-Tuareg forces strike Mali capital Bamako killing Defense Minister in major Sahel escalation

item.title: FIMI & Cognitive Warfare Monitor — W/E 30 April 2026
item.description: No new FIMI operations identified this week

item.title: Global Environmental Risks Monitor — W/E 30 April 2026
item.description: AMOC weakening trajectory accelerated 60% beyond model projections

item.title: European Strategic Autonomy Monitor — W/E 29 April 2026
item.description: EU sanctions Russian FIMI entities Euromore and Pravfond for influence operations

item.title: Global Macro Monitor — W/E 29 April 2026
Artificial Intelligence Monitor — W/E 27 April 2026
Global Macro Monitor — W/E 27 April 2026
World Democracy Monitor — W/E 27 April 2026
item.description: United States has crossed threshold into electoral democracy status with structural risks to multiple democratic pillars

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Monitor — W/E 24 April 2026 (Re-issued)
item.description: Re-issued 2026-04-24 per AD-2026-04-24l-instance-1. Original publish shipped 5 developments; K0-pass2 lab evidence produced 14.

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Monitor — W/E 21 April 2026
item.description: The EU AI Act standards vacuum represents the most immediate governance risk as high-risk compliance deadline approaches without sufficient harmonised standards

item.title: Global Macro Monitor — W/E 21 April 2026
item.description: Tariff shock has transitioned from uncertainty to structural competitiveness drag

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — W/E 20 April 2026
item.description: Hungary exemplifies autocratisation from within via institutional capture

item.title: Strategic Conflict & Escalation Monitor — W/E 19 April 2026
item.description: Direct military exchanges between Iran-US-Israel on Apr 19 with Houthi Red Sea threats risk regional widening

item.title: Strategic Conflict & Escalation Monitor — W/E 18 April 2026
item.description: Hormuz closure constitutes largest oil disruption risk amid sustained I2/I4 Red levels despite F3/F4 flags

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — W/E 18 April 2026
item.description: US faces rapid institutional decay with vdem_ert_active across multiple pillars

item.title: Global Environmental Risks Monitor — W/E 18 April 2026
item.description: Six planetary boundaries remain transgressed with no incremental shifts this week per Tier 1 confirmation

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Monitor — W/E 17 April 2026
Global Macro Monitor — W/E 17 April 2026
item.description: The Iran war and closure of the Strait of Hormuz represents the most significant supply shock to the global oil market in history, with 20% of global supply disrupted

item.title: European Strategic Autonomy Monitor — W/E 16 April 2026
item.description: EU Council adopts offensive hybrid deterrence conclusions as EDIP programme opens first industry calls.

item.title: FIMI & Cognitive Warfare Monitor — W/E 16 April 2026
item.description: No new FIMI operations identified this week across six tracked actors; quiet week reflects post-election lull and detection gaps in AI-embedded campaigns.

item.title: Global Macro Monitor — W/E 14 April 2026
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz closure represents a structural supply shock rather than a temporary disruption, with lasting impacts on global energy markets

item.title: Strategic Conflict & Escalation Monitor — W/E 13 April 2026
item.description: No dominant conflict signal identified; all theatres stable with no new Tier 1 verified developments.

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — W/E 13 April 2026
item.description: US institutional capture accelerates autocratization risking FIMI vulnerability

item.title: European Strategic Autonomy Monitor — W/E 12 April 2026
item.description: EU Council adopts conclusions to counter Russia-linked hybrid threats including sabotage and FIMI.

item.title: Strategic Conflict & Escalation Monitor — W/E 12 April 2026
item.description: No verified escalation signals exceed baseline in provided documents this week.

item.title: FIMI & Cognitive Warfare Monitor — W/E 12 April 2026
item.description: No new FIMI operations identified this week across six tracked actors; quiet week reflects post-election lull and detection gaps in AI-embedded campaigns.

item.title: Global Macro Monitor — W/E 12 April 2026
World Democracy Monitor — W/E 12 April 2026
Global Environmental Risks Monitor — W/E 12 April 2026
item.description: Planetary boundary trajectories stable with no new Tier 1 signals

item.title: Global Environmental Risks Monitor — W/E 11 April 2026
item.description: Environmental risks remain the dominant long-term threat across all nine planetary boundaries, with six boundaries transgressed and two in high-risk status. Short-term risk perception has shifted towa

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Monitor — W/E 10 April 2026
item.description: EU AI Act Standards Vacuum enables compliance arbitrage until August 2026

item.title: European Strategic Autonomy Monitor — W/E 8 April 2026
item.description: EU Council adopts hybrid deterrence conclusions as Russia's grey-zone tempo accelerates. 40 arson plots Germany/Poland (quadrupled 2023-2024), airspace incursions, ECFR offensive doctrine shift. Hungary election 4 days out.

item.title: GMM Issue 3 — Liberation Day Cascade: Global Macro Stress Escalates to CRITICAL
World Democracy Monitor — W/E 6 April 2026
item.description: Hungary votes on April 12 with Tisza polling 19–23 points ahead of Fidesz — the most consequential EU electoral test in a decade — while Benin holds a closed presidential contest on the same day and the US executive-SCOTUS confrontation enters a new phase.

item.title: Environmental Risks Monitor — W/E 4 April 2026
item.description: Planetary Health Check 2025 reports ocean acidification boundary breached; 7/9 planetary boundaries exceeded, increasing probability of multi-domain cascade risk.

item.title: FIMI & Cognitive Warfare Monitor — W/E 2 April 2026
item.description: Maintenance update: no new Tier-1 FIMI intelligence this week. Cross-monitor signal updated — Artificial Intelligence Monitor flags agentic autonomous narrative attacks as confirmed emerging capability class.

item.title: European Strategic Autonomy Monitor — W/E 1 April 2026
item.description: Russian spring offensive stalling as internal critics admit Ukrainian counterattacks 'rather successful'; Kremlin halts gasoline exports; Zelensky proposes Easter ceasefire — dismissed by Moscow. Hungary election enters final 12 days. SAFE second wave €17.1B for France and Czechia.

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Monitor — W/E 1 April 2026
item.description: Anthropic's double opsec failure in five days — Claude Mythos leaked via CMS and KAIROS/BUDDY/undercover-mode exposed via npm — reveals systemic internal controls failure at the lab with the largest DoD-adjacent AI footprint, exactly as the GSA 'any lawful use' clause threatens to codify no-safety-restrictions as standard federal AI procurement.

item.title: FIMI & Cognitive Warfare Monitor — W/E 1 April 2026
item.description: Storm-1516 deploys Euronews impersonation against Hungary as the April 12 election enters its critical phase — three simultaneous confirmed or assessed Russian operations now targeting the same election, the highest documented pre-election FIMI density for a single EU member state.

item.title: Global Macro Monitor — W/E 1 April 2026
item.description: Liberation Day tariffs confirm worst-case trade scenario at Smoot-Hawley levels; private credit gate events trigger at Ares and Apollo; consumer confidence breaks below the GFC trough to 53.3 — three structural risks that were flagged as probable in prior weeks are now confirmed.

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — W/E 1 April 2026
item.description: Hungary enters the final 11 days with aggregate polling at 50.8% for Tisza — a potential end to 16 years of illiberal rule — while the US Supreme Court heard oral arguments testing whether the 14th Amendment citizenship clause can be reinterpreted by executive order alone.

item.title: Strategic Conflict & Escalation Monitor — W/E 30 March 2026
item.description: Sudan drives the week's highest escalation signal — RSF capture of Kurmuk displaced 73,000 people in a single event, with indicators for rhetoric, economic warfare, diplomacy, and displacement all above initial baseline simultaneously.

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Monitor — Week of 30 March 2026
item.description: China's state formalisation of AI tokens as an economic settlement unit and OpenAI's compute reallocation from video synthesis to robotics pre-training are this week's two structural signals — geopolitical AI architecture is now being built in terminology committees as much as in data centers.

item.title: Global FIMI & Cognitive Warfare Monitor — W/E 30 March 2026
item.description: Russia's Social Design Agency confirmed operating from the Budapest Embassy ahead of Hungary's April 12 election; Meta's largest-ever Russia-attributed CIB takedown; Turning Point Action's Alliance of Sovereign Nations operational across European far-right.

item.title: Macro Monitor — TL;DR Week of 30 March 2026
item.description: System stress at ELEVATED -> HIGH with zero bullish asset class scores: consumer confidence at a GFC-trough, a Hormuz supply disruption embedding a $15-20 oil premium, and private credit gate risk the most underpriced systemic trigger in the model.

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — 30 March 2026
item.description: Tisza leads Fidesz by 23 points with 13 days to Hungary's April 12 election; Georgia's new March 2026 law package completes its civil society capture architecture; and the Philippines enters the heatmap as a new Rapid Decay entry after CIVICUS adds it to its Watchlist at Repressed rating.

item.title: European Geopolitical & Hybrid Threat Monitor -- W/E 29 March 2026
item.description: Hungary election crisis deepens with BBC voter intimidation expose and confirmed Russian political technologists in Budapest; NATO reports 20% European defence spending increase; Slovenia election produces razor-thin result amid Israeli Black Cube interference.

item.title: Global Environmental Risks & Planetary Boundaries Monitor — W/E 29 March 2026
item.description: AMOC freshwater flux anomaly enters a third consecutive week — the strongest sustained early-warning signal since the FovS proxy metric was established — while Thwaites glacier grounding line retreat exceeds IPCC upper projections and Amazon vegetation stress expands to 450,000 km².
